violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. FMC expired on 01/12/2025. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

Raw animal foods (fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (sauce) in the prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Chicken wings and rice, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. See disposal. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Chicken tenders and taquitos were observed held at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Rice and chicken wings were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment to sanitize in 3 bay sink until serviced and dispensing proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

Lowboy #1 was not in good repair, ambient observed at 47F. PIC took unit out of service at time of inspection. TCS foods not to be stored in unit until serviced and has a temperature of 41F or below. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
The floor in the fryer area is in poor repair, inspector observed missing tiles with exposed concrete. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Written procedures were given to the person in charge at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

Raw animal foods (such as fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

Chimichangas, which were prepared in advance, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Chimichangas were voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Proper cooling methods were reviewed with the person in charge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chimichangas were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling techniques were reviewed with the person in charge at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

The prep sink and bar ice well are lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Rice and tamales were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

A glue trap with insects were observed in the basement. Dead rodents, birds, and insects must be removed promptly from the facilities to ensure clean and sanitary facilities and to preclude exacerbating the situation by allowing carcasses to attract other pests. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items moved to warewashing area to be washed/rinsed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

Salsa, cheese, eggs and other items were observed held at 49-53 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler. The walk-in cooler was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 63 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All TCS foods held in the unit overnight was voluntarily disposed of by PIC. Unit to not be used until it can be serviced/replaced/repaired. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

Rice, beans and other items observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

Cans of restricted aerosolized insecticides were observed within the establishment. Restricted aerosolized insecticides shall not be used within the establishment by staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

The walk-in cooler observed in poor repair (maintaining ambient temperature of 63 degrees Fahrenheit). Unit is to not be used until it is repaired/replaced/serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with: 1) The inside of the prep units along the cookline and in the prep area observed with water pooling at the bottom shelf of the unit 2) The fan near the prep sink observed with an accumulation of dust. 3) The ice machine observed with biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023
